top_signals.title: Qatar Quietly Sends LNG Tanker Through Hormuz Despite Tensions
top_signals.summary: Another liquefied natural gas shipment from Qatar transited the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, according to ship data, even as tension in the region threatens a broader peace deal between the US and Iran.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Sanctioned tanker scrapping could offset large order book: operators
top_signals.summary: Whether a large newbuild order book would weigh on tanker rates could depend on the pace of shadow-fleet scrapping and evolving demand situations during the Hormuz shipping crisis, some ship operators said. top_signals.title: Carriers announce peak season surcharges on demand surge ahead of fresh US tariffs
top_signals.summary: Container shipping majors are out with peak season surcharges for mid-June and early-July, driven by soaring demand amid an early onset of peak season as US importers rush to get goods into the country before Section 301 tariffs take effect. top_signals.title: Spot bitcoin ETFs log $1.7 billion in weekly outflows, largest since February 2025
top_signals.summary: The outflows were primarily driven by macroeconomic headlines, especially the stronger-than-expected U.S. jobs report, one analyst said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Melina Travlos: “Greek Owners Are Leading Fleet Renewal and Environmental Performance”

top_signals.title: Why Solar Power Is Booming Under Trump
top_signals.summary: Despite the Trump administration’s pivot away from renewable energies, solar continues to dominate new energy additions in the United States. Newly released data from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) shows that at the close of last year, solar energy additions were the single largest form of new energy capacity installations for the 28th straight month, starting in September of 2023. In fact, in spite of a broad rollback of Biden-era clean energy incentives since Trump resumed office in January of last year, renewables represented…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: VLCCs: Market Relatively Flat Last Week
top_signals.summary: Clean LR2 The TC1 75kt MEG/Japan index dropped an incremental 6.39 points to WS510 this week. A voyage west also came down with the TC20 90kt MEG/UK-Continent index going from $10.16 million to $9.87 million. The TC15 80kt Mediterranean/East index came down a further $680,000 this week to $4.47 million with the corresponding TCE dropping ...

top_signals.title: Greek shipowners work with Japan, Liberia on new IMO bunker rules
top_signals.summary: The Union of Greek Shipowners has reached out to Liberia and Japan to work on alternative proposals to the International Maritime Organization’s Net-Zero Framework, the group said June 5, also calling on the EU to make firmer commitments in rolling back its regulations on maritime greenhouse gas emissions. top_signals.title: Geography-Washing: The NGO Shipbreaking Platform Finds Yet Another Country to Disapprove Of
top_signals.summary: The Usual Answer: Ban It There is a certain comfort in consistency, and the NGO Shipbreaking Platform offers it in abundance. Whatever the question, the answer is a ban. top_signals.title: Dry Bulk Market: Capesize Market Softening
top_signals.summary: Capesize The market closed the week on a softer footing, with the BCI 182 5TC easing from just under $50,000 at the start of the week to $44,374, reflecting a clear loss of momentum as the week progressed.
